title: Quantitative Reasoning, Estimation, and Problem Framing · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Estimate attendance, supplies, time, and uncertainty for a neighborhood event before exact values are available. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a transparent event-planning estimate notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning.
Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.
Translate a practical question into known quantities, unknown quantities, constraints, and operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Quantity Meaning evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Quantity Meaning assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Interpret a number together with its unit, reference population, time window, and practical meaning. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner quantitative reasoning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Quantity Meaning, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a transparent event-planning estimate notebook.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A bare number has the same meaning in every context. This fails the Quantity Meaning contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Assumption Record applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.

hints: Name the Assumption Record precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Quantity Meaning to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Quantity Meaning.

checks.description: The decision applies Estimation to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Estimation.

checks.description: The decision applies Problem Translation to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Problem Translation.

checks.description: The decision applies Reasonableness to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Reasonableness.

checks.description: The decision applies Assumption Record to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Assumption Record.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using state and assumptions plus specific case evidence.
